text:
i graded 52 MCP servers. the best one has 1 tool.

Google Colab MCP: execute_code. that's it. A+ (97.3/100). 92 tokens.

GitHub official: 80 tools. 20,444 tokens. F.

Notation official: 22 tools. 4,463 tokens. F.

more isn't better.

{url}

the design philosophy behind Google's Colab MCP is interesting.

1 tool: execute_code(code: str). the LLM writes Python instead of calling 80 specific endpoints.

that's not a limitation. it's the whole point.

vs Notion: 22 tools including one called API-post-block-children.

{url}

MCP server quality: article about Colab A+ vs Notion F.

same protocol. 48x more tokens. opposite grades.

If you're building an MCP server — what's your execute_code equivalent?

{url}
